note_evidence: his illness began with anemia, which prompted evaluation revealing a colonic polyp
rationale: Note states anemia began the illness and prompted workup.

note_evidence: revealing a colonic polyp and subsequently a colon tumor
rationale: Colon cancer diagnosed after polyp then tumor.

note_evidence: he elected to forgo further cancer-directed treatment
rationale: Patient declined further cancer treatment for comfort care.

note_evidence: history of ischemic heart disease with abnormal cardiac and coronary imaging
rationale: Ischemic heart disease with abnormal imaging documented.

note_evidence: chest tightness relieved by sublingual nitroglycerin spray, which he uses approximately twice weekly, typically triggered by exertion (e.g., stairs) or emotional stress, with relief in a few minutes
rationale: All angina details documented.

note_evidence: intermittent dull, heavy sensation in the lower abdomen; not severe
rationale: Matches dull, heavy, low-intensity lower abdominal pain.

note_evidence: Fatigue: predominant complaint
rationale: Fatigue is predominant symptom.

note_evidence: Fatigue, decreased appetite, and increased somnolence — most consistent with combined effects of advanced malignancy, cardiac disease, and anemia
rationale: Decreased appetite and increased napping attributed to those conditions.

note_evidence: Clopidogrel 75 mg PO once daily
rationale: Clopidogrel dose/route/freq continued unchanged.

note_evidence: Simvastatin 20 mg PO nightly
rationale: Simvastatin details continued unchanged.

note_evidence: Metoprolol (long-acting) 100 mg PO daily
rationale: Metoprolol LA 100 mg continued unchanged.

note_evidence: Nitroglycerin spray 0.4 mg SL PRN chest pain
rationale: Nitroglycerin spray details continued unchanged.

note_evidence: Clopidogrel and simvastatin ... identified as candidates for future deprescribing if pill burden becomes burdensome
rationale: Meds unchanged now; those two flagged for future deprescribing.

note_evidence: No further laboratory monitoring (e.g., blood draws) unless a result would change comfort management
rationale: Stop labs unless would change comfort documented.

note_evidence: Initial hospice admission assessment
rationale: Initial hospice assessment performed.

note_evidence: Individualized plan of care to be written incorporating patient's stated goals and preferences
rationale: Plan of care to be written/finalized rather than already developed, though goals documented.

note_evidence: Comfort-focused hospice care aligned with patient's stated priorities
rationale: Hospice care initiated.

note_evidence: two-physician certification (attending and hospice physician) completed
rationale: Two-physician certification completed.

note_evidence: Formal notifications to primary care physician and Medicare to be handled by hospice team
rationale: Notifications to PCP and Medicare documented.

note_evidence: RN visits ~2×/week to begin; first follow-up scheduled Thursday
rationale: Nursing twice weekly, next Thursday.

note_evidence: Home health aide for bathing — declined at this time at patient's request; may be added later
rationale: Aide offered, declined, may be added later.

note_evidence: Chaplain and social worker services offered and available as desired
rationale: Chaplain and social worker offered.

note_evidence: family instructed to call hospice first (not 911/ambulance) for chest pain or acute events
rationale: Counseled to call hospice line first for chest pain.

note_evidence: Respite care available to support caregiver; discussed with wife
rationale: Respite care explained to support caregiver.

note_evidence: DNR / no resuscitation, no mechanical ventilation, no emergency transport
rationale: DNR/DNI and no heroic measures documented.

note_evidence: Time on the porch in the mornings, listening to his jazz records in the afternoon, his wife's cooking, and quiet
rationale: Good-day goals documented.

note_evidence: Socially isolated by preference; content with a small social circle
rationale: Social isolation with contentment documented.

note_evidence: Married 51 years; wife is primary caregiver and manages his medications
rationale: Lives with wife of 51 years, primary caregiver managing meds.

note_evidence: Wife to maintain a daily comfort diary (pain, appetite, sleep)
rationale: Caregiver comfort diary tracking pain, appetite, sleep.

note_evidence: physical exam, cardiac/pulmonary auscultation, skin assessment, and home safety evaluation to be performed by admitting nurse following this discussion; findings not yet available
rationale: History documented but physical exam not yet performed.

note_evidence: Symptom-directed care for pain, dyspnea, chest tightness, and anxiety
rationale: Symptom-focused plan for pain, breathlessness, chest tightness, anxiety.
